name: Palatine German
notes: STUB (deliberate): the grapheme inventory of Palatine German is NOT specified. Pälzisch/Pfälzisch is a West Central German (Rhine Franconian) dialect group. It has NO published spelling norm and no letter->IPA table exists in any consultable source: it is written ad hoc in German spelling, which does not encode the dialect's lenition of /p t k/, its monophthongisation, or its vowel qualities. Documented facts that a future spec could build on, but which are not a map: rounded front vowels are absent (Standard German ö -> [e], ü -> [i], e.g. 'kenne' for 'können'); MHG ei surfaces as [ɛɪ̯], written <ää> (Schdää 'Stein'); intervocalic <g> -> [ç] (Nachel 'Nagel'); the written convention <gh-> is used to keep morphology visible (ghowe = gehoben). Building a map would require inventing IPA values, so none is claimed. INPUT CONTRACT (for any future spec): Latin-script, German-convention Palatine text. The closely related Pennsylvania German (pdc) DOES have a citable norm (Buffington-Barba) and is specced.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Pfälzische Dialekte
sources.notes: Consulted: gives no orthography table and no phoneme chart, only prose correspondences (no ö/ü; ei -> [ɛɪ̯] written <ää>; intervocalic g -> [ç]; the <gh-> writing convention).

ancestors.notes: Germanic — the classification node this language sits under.
